intake_questions.text: List all in-scope IP ranges, domains, sub-domains, and applications with their production/staging status
What is the authentication model in use (SSO, OAuth2/OIDC, API keys, JWT, Basic Auth, SAML)?
Are there WAF, IDS/IPS, rate-limiting, or bot-detection controls in front of in-scope targets?
What is the agreed testing window (start/end datetime, timezone) and is there a blackout period during business hours?
Are staging or production systems in scope? Who is the emergency contact for critical findings during the test?
Are there any cloud environments (AWS/Azure/GCP) or container platforms (Kubernetes/Docker) in scope?
What mobile platforms (iOS/Android) or thick-client applications are in scope and are API endpoints shared with the web app?
Have any previous penetration tests been conducted? If so, were all critical/high findings from the last test remediated?

scope_template.prompt: Add each in-scope domain, sub-domain, or IP range (one per entry)
Define what is explicitly in scope (web app, API, mobile, infrastructure, cloud)
List systems, paths, or attack types explicitly excluded from testing (e.g. DoS, social engineering)
Document agreed testing depth, credential access level, and any white/grey/black-box status
Record rules of engagement: rate-limit tolerance, maximum request rate, notification requirements
Note any compliance requirements that constrain testing (PCI DSS, DPDP data handling during test)

requirements.text: Pre-engagement: Written authorisation, rules of engagement, scope document, emergency contacts, and testing window must all be confirmed and signed before testing begins.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Signed scope document; rules of engagement; written authorisation; emergency contact sheet; testing window confirmation

requirements.text: Passive and active information gathering: DNS enumeration, WHOIS, certificate transparency, sub-domain enumeration, Shodan/Censys exposure, BGP/ASN lookup, and service fingerprinting.
requirements.evidence_expectation: dns.txt, cert_transparency.txt, subdomain_enum.txt, shodan_results.txt, nmap service scan output

requirements.text: Technology stack identification and CVE correlation: identify all web servers, frameworks, CMS versions, JavaScript libraries, and third-party components; correlate with NVD/Exploit-DB.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Technology fingerprint report (Wappalyzer/whatweb output); CVE correlation table for identified versions; dependency scan results

requirements.text: HTTP security headers review: verify presence and correctness of HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy on all in-scope endpoints.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Security headers audit report; CSP policy review; curl response header dump for all tested endpoints

requirements.text: TLS/SSL configuration review: protocol versions, cipher suites, certificate validity, certificate chain trust, HSTS max-age, OCSP stapling, and certificate pinning where applicable.
requirements.evidence_expectation: testssl.sh or SSLyze full output for all HTTPS endpoints; certificate details; grading result

requirements.text: Server and application configuration: identify default credentials, unnecessary services, exposed admin/debug interfaces, directory listing, HTTP methods allowed, and verbose error messages.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Service enumeration output; admin interface discovery; HTTP method test results; nikto or automated scanner output

requirements.text: Cloud and container security: assess cloud storage bucket permissions (public S3/GCS/Blob), misconfigured IAM roles, exposed cloud metadata endpoints, and container registry security.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Cloud misconfiguration scan output (ScoutSuite, Prowler, or manual); bucket policy review; IAM permission review; metadata endpoint test results

requirements.text: Authentication mechanism testing: brute-force protection, account lockout policy, credential stuffing resilience, password complexity enforcement, and common/breached password rejection.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Authentication test results; lockout policy verification; credential stuffing resilience test; breached password check evidence

requirements.text: Password reset and account recovery: insecure token generation, token entropy, token expiry enforcement, host header injection in reset emails, and account enumeration via reset flow.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Password reset flow test results; token entropy analysis; host header injection test; account enumeration test evidence

requirements.text: Multi-factor authentication security: MFA bypass techniques (code reuse, response manipulation, backup code abuse), session handling after MFA, and out-of-band verifier security (OTP SMS/email).
requirements.evidence_expectation: MFA bypass test results; OTP reuse test; response manipulation test; backup code security review

requirements.text: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC / JWT security: state parameter validation, redirect_uri whitelist bypass, implicit flow token leakage, JWT algorithm confusion (none/RS256 to HS256), weak JWT secrets, token replay.
requirements.evidence_expectation: OAuth flow test results; JWT analysis (jwt.io/token inspection); algorithm confusion test; weak secret brute-force attempt

requirements.text: Horizontal and vertical privilege escalation: IDOR/BOLA (cross-account object access), BFLA (admin function access by low-privilege user), role boundary enforcement, and insecure direct object references.
requirements.evidence_expectation: IDOR test cases with cross-account access proof; admin function access test results; privilege escalation evidence; role comparison matrix

requirements.text: Mass assignment and object property exposure: test whether API endpoints accept and persist non-whitelisted properties (role, admin, balance) via additional body parameters in PUT/PATCH/POST requests.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Mass assignment test cases; response comparison showing property persistence; role elevation via mass assignment proof-of-concept

requirements.text: SQL injection testing: classic, blind (boolean and time-based), out-of-band, and second-order injection across all user-controlled inputs in forms, URL parameters, headers, and JSON/XML bodies.
requirements.evidence_expectation: SQLMap output or manual SQLi proof; database version/schema extracted; affected parameters list; payloads used

requirements.text: Cross-site scripting: reflected, stored, and DOM-based XSS across all user-controlled inputs. Test CSP bypass and document all affected parameters with proof-of-concept payloads.
requirements.evidence_expectation: XSS test results with PoC payloads; CSP bypass attempts; DOM XSS source-sink analysis; affected parameter list

requirements.text: Command injection and template injection: OS command injection (blind and direct), SSTI across all template engines (Jinja2, Twig, FreeMarker, Smarty), and expression language injection.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Command injection test results; SSTI payload responses; affected template/parameter identification

requirements.text: XML External Entity (XXE) injection: test all XML-accepting endpoints and document upload features for external entity processing, blind XXE via out-of-band channels, and XXE-based SSRF.
requirements.evidence_expectation: XXE test payloads and responses; file read proof-of-concept (/etc/passwd); blind XXE DNS/HTTP callback confirmation

requirements.text: Deserialization vulnerabilities: test for insecure object deserialization in Java (ysoserial), .NET (YSoSerial.net), PHP (POP chains), and Python pickle to identify RCE or DoS vectors.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Deserialization test payloads; evidence of deserialized objects in traffic; RCE proof-of-concept where applicable

requirements.text: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF): test all URL-accepting inputs (webhooks, file import, PDF generators, image fetch) for SSRF to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, and blind SSRF.
requirements.evidence_expectation: SSRF test results; internal service access proof; cloud metadata access (169.254.169.254); Burp Collaborator/interactsh DNS/HTTP callbacks

requirements.text: File upload security: web shell upload bypass (extension filtering, MIME-type spoofing, double extension), storage location verification, file execution prevention, malware upload capability, and zip-slip.
requirements.evidence_expectation: File upload test results; bypass technique evidence; storage path disclosure; web shell upload proof-of-concept (non-executed)

requirements.text: Session token security: entropy analysis (Burp Sequencer), secure/httponly/samesite flag configuration, logout invalidation, session fixation, idle timeout, and concurrent session handling.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Session token analysis report; Burp Sequencer output; flag verification; logout invalidation test; fixation test results

requirements.text: CORS policy testing: verify that Access-Control-Allow-Origin does not reflect arbitrary origins, does not use wildcards with credentials, and that pre-flight requests are validated server-side.
requirements.evidence_expectation: CORS test results showing reflected origin; credentials with CORS misconfiguration PoC; pre-flight bypass attempts

requirements.text: API authentication and authorisation: BOLA (cross-account object access), BFLA (function-level privilege), broken object property-level auth, and mass assignment across all API endpoints.
requirements.evidence_expectation: API endpoint inventory; BOLA/BFLA test cases; mass assignment results; cross-account access proof

requirements.text: API rate limiting, resource consumption, and excessive data exposure: verify rate limits enforced per endpoint, excessive data fields not returned, and no unrestricted bulk data export possible.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Rate limiting test results; data exposure analysis comparing response fields against minimum required; bulk export test

requirements.text: API versioning and shadow API discovery: enumerate all API versions accessible, test whether deprecated versions are still active, and fuzz for undocumented endpoints via path discovery.
requirements.evidence_expectation: API version inventory; deprecated version access test; undocumented endpoint discovery results (path fuzzing output)

requirements.text: GraphQL security: introspection enabled in production, nested query DoS (deep recursion), field suggestion abuse, GraphQL injection, unauthorised mutation access, and batching attacks.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Introspection query results; deep query test; mutation authorisation test; GraphQL injection test; batching abuse test

requirements.text: WebSocket security: authentication enforcement on upgrade request, message-level authorisation, injection via WebSocket messages (SQLi, XSS, CSWSH), and cross-origin WebSocket hijacking.
requirements.evidence_expectation: WebSocket upgrade auth test; message injection test results; CSWSH proof-of-concept; cross-origin hijacking attempt

requirements.text: Credentials and secret exposure: identify hardcoded secrets in client-side JS, exposed git repositories (.git), environment files (.env), backup files, API keys in HTML/JS source, and sensitive data in responses.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Secret scanning results (truffleHog/gitleaks output); exposed file discovery; hardcoded credential evidence in source; sensitive data in API responses

requirements.text: Open redirect and URL manipulation: test redirect parameters for unvalidated redirects to external domains, parameter-based open redirect in OAuth flows, and header injection via redirect parameters.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Open redirect test results; OAuth redirect_uri bypass test; affected parameter list; redirect to attacker-controlled domain PoC

requirements.text: Network and infrastructure security: firewall rule validation, network segmentation testing, exposed management interfaces, default service credentials, and lateral movement potential from web-tier.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Network scan output; firewall bypass test results; management interface discovery; default credential test; segmentation verification evidence

requirements.text: Business logic flaws: price/quantity manipulation, workflow step skipping, negative value abuse, race conditions in critical operations (purchase, transfer, coupon redemption), and limit bypass.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Business logic test cases and outcomes; price manipulation PoC; race condition evidence (concurrent request results); workflow bypass proof

requirements.text: Dependency and supply chain security: identify all third-party libraries, frameworks, and plugins; correlate versions against CVE databases; identify outdated, end-of-life, or malicious packages.
requirements.evidence_expectation: OWASP Dependency-Check/Snyk/npm audit output; CVE table for identified packages; EOL component list; SCA scan results

requirements.text: Remediation retest: verify that all critical and high findings from the initial assessment have been remediated. Perform targeted retesting of specific fixes and document retest status for each finding.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Retest report per finding; fix verification screenshots; before/after comparison; updated risk status for each retested finding

task_templates.title: Kick-off call — scope confirmation, rules of engagement, and emergency contacts
Pre-engagement documentation review — authorisation, RoE, and scope sign-off
Phase 01 — Passive OSINT and DNS reconnaissance (PT-Orc Phase 01)
Phase 02 — IP analysis, network mapping, and service enumeration (PT-Orc Phase 02)
Phase 03 — Comprehensive vulnerability scan — authenticated and unauthenticated (PT-Orc Phase 03)
Phase 04 — TLS/SSL configuration review and certificate analysis (PT-Orc Phase 04)
Phase 05 — Web enumeration — directory bruteforce, technology fingerprinting, CVE scan (PT-Orc Phase 05)
Phase 06 — CMS and WordPress security assessment (PT-Orc Phase 06)
Phase 07 — Service-level verification and default credential testing (PT-Orc Phase 07)
Phase 08 — Application and API security review — auth, authz, injection, XSS, SSRF (PT-Orc Phase 08)
Phase 09 — AI/LLM component review if applicable (PT-Orc Phase 09)
Manual testing — IDOR/BOLA, business logic, race conditions, mass assignment
File upload, XXE, deserialization, and SSRF targeted testing
CORS, open redirect, WebSocket, and GraphQL security testing
Secret scanning — client-side JS, exposed .git, .env, backup files
Cloud and container security review — bucket permissions, IAM, metadata endpoints
Dependency and supply chain analysis — SCA scan and CVE correlation
Internal findings review, deduplication, and CVSS scoring
Findings walkthrough with client technical team
Retest of critical and high severity findings post-remediation
Final report QA — evidence completeness, CVSS accuracy, remediation guidance review

severity_model.critical: CVSS 9.0-10.0: Direct exploitation with no authentication, remote code execution, full data breach, or complete system compromise. Requires immediate escalation and emergency notification to the client.
severity_model.high: CVSS 7.0-8.9: Significant vulnerability that is directly exploitable, leads to substantial data exposure, privilege escalation, or authentication bypass. Must be remediated before report release.
severity_model.medium: CVSS 4.0-6.9: Exploitable vulnerability requiring specific conditions, user interaction, or limited access. Poses meaningful risk in combination with other issues. Remediate within 30 days.
severity_model.low: CVSS 0.1-3.9: Minimal direct impact. Low risk independently but may contribute to a chain attack. Remediate within 90 days.
severity_model.informational: No CVSS score. Defence-in-depth observation, best-practice enhancement, or security hygiene recommendation with no immediate exploitability.

review_gates.label: Scope and rules of engagement approved
Active testing evidence complete and verified
Findings reviewed and CVSS severity confirmed
Retest evidence accepted and report updated

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Authentication Hardening Workshop
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review current authentication architecture against OWASP ASVS Level 2. Cover MFA implementation, password policy, JWT security, and OAuth2/OIDC best practices. Produce a prioritised hardening roadmap.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: API Security Architecture Review
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Walk through the API design against OWASP API Security Top 10 (2023). Cover authorisation model (BOLA/BFLA prevention), rate limiting strategy, schema validation, versioning policy, and API gateway configuration.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Security Headers and TLS Hardening Advisory
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review and configure Content Security Policy, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and remaining security headers. Review TLS cipher suite configuration and certificate management. Produce configuration snippets for each web server/platform in use.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Injection Prevention and Secure Coding Workshop
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Cover parameterised queries, ORM usage, output encoding, template sandboxing, and file upload validation. Review relevant OWASP Cheat Sheets. Conduct focused code review of the highest-risk input processing areas.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Dependency Management and Supply Chain Security Advisory
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review dependency update cadence. Establish SCA tooling in CI/CD pipeline (OWASP Dependency-Check, Snyk, Dependabot). Define severity thresholds that block builds. Review container base image patching policy.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Cloud Security Posture Review
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review cloud IAM policies, storage bucket permissions, security group configurations, and logging coverage. Run CSPM assessment (ScoutSuite/Prowler). Produce prioritised cloud hardening checklist mapped to CIS Benchmarks.

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Remediation Prioritisation and Roadmap Workshop
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Work through all identified findings with the development team. Prioritise by business impact and CVSS score. Assign owners, define remediation approaches, and set realistic timelines. Produce an action plan for executive review.
